queries.query: What is the default ef_search value for HNSW indexes in pgvector?
expected_snippets: _ef_search_ value is set to 40 (which is the default)
the default hnsw.ef_search of 40, only 4 rows will match on average

queries.query: By how much does Contextual Retrieval combined with reranking reduce the top-20-chunk retrieval failure rate?
expected_snippets: when combined with reranking, by 67%
top-20-chunk retrieval failure rate by 67% (5.7% → 1.9%)

queries.query: What MRR@10 does SPLADE-v3 achieve on the MS MARCO dev set?
expected_snippets: gets more than 40 MRR @ 10 on the MS MARCO dev set

queries.query: What embedding dimension does Qwen3-Embedding-8B produce by default?
expected_snippets: Qwen3-Embedding-8B/4096 achieves Top-3 accuracy
Qwen-3-Embedding-8B: 8B parameters, 4096 dimensions

queries.query: What new index scan feature was introduced in pgvector 0.8.0 to fix partial results when combining HNSW with WHERE clause filters?
expected_snippets: Added support for iterative index scans
Check if enough results are returned. If not, repeat the scan.

queries.query: How does Hypothetical Document Embedding (HyDE) work and what is the averaging step?
expected_snippets: this is done five times
encodes each hypothetical document into an embedding vector and averages them

queries.query: What is the required minimum value of ef_construction relative to m when building an HNSW index?
expected_snippets: _ef_construction_ is required to be 2x the value of _m_

queries.query: Around what embedding dimension does Matryoshka Representation Learning hit a sweet spot for Qwen3-Embedding-0.6B on retrieval quality vs storage cost?
expected_snippets: around the early 400 range is a good trade off
I will be using a 512 (for some reason I like this number)

queries.query: What are the tradeoffs between cross-encoder rerankers and bi-encoder retrievers in a two-stage retrieval pipeline?
expected_snippets: cross-encoders that jointly process a pair of query and document
fast bi-encoder retrieval followed by accurate cross-encoder reranking

queries.query: How does hybrid search combining dense and sparse retrieval improve retrieval quality compared to using either alone, especially for out-of-domain queries?
expected_snippets: the two are complementary in how they model relevance
especially when applied to out-of-domain datasets
combining the embeddings and BM25 techniques

queries.query: What is late chunking and how does it preserve contextual information across chunk boundaries compared to traditional chunk-then-embed approaches?
expected_snippets: only afterwards dividing these contextually-rich embeddings into chunks
Segmentation boundaries are then applied to these context-aware representations

queries.query: What is Contextual Retrieval and what two sub-techniques does it combine to reduce retrieval failures?
expected_snippets: uses two sub-techniques: Contextual Embeddings and Contextual BM25
prepending chunk-specific explanatory context to each chunk before embedding

queries.query: How does SPLADE represent text as sparse embeddings and what makes it more effective than BM25 for neural information retrieval?
expected_snippets: SPLADE-v3 vastly outperforms BM25
Sparse embedding: Lexical weights for each token in the vocabulary

queries.query: What HNSW parameters control the tradeoff between index build speed, index quality, and query accuracy?
expected_snippets: index quality tradeoff is controlled via the efConstruction parameter
modifying three parameters: `M`, `efSearch`, and `efConstruction`

queries.query: How does LLM performance degrade with increasing context length and what factors influence the rate of degradation?
expected_snippets: model performance degrades more significantly with increasing input length
needle-question pairs increases the rate of performance degradation

queries.query: What are the tradeoffs between fixed-size, semantic, and LLM-guided chunking strategies for RAG retrieval quality?
expected_snippets: (64-128 tokens) are optimal for datasets with concise, fact-based answers
ClusterSemanticChunker , which uses any given embedding model
Lumber Chunker performs best for in-document retrieval

queries.query: How do Reciprocal Rank Fusion and convex combination score fusion differ in their behavior and which is more suitable for hybrid retrieval?
expected_snippets: we find RRF to be sensitive to its parameters
convex combination outperforms RRF in in-domain and out-of-domain settings
tuned RRF generalizes poorly to out-of-domain datasets

queries.query: How do Qwen3 embedding models compare to BGE-M3 and GTE models across standard retrieval benchmarks like MTEB?
expected_snippets: 70.58 on the MTEB Multilingual benchmark
Qwen-3 models consistently outperform BGE-M3 across all evaluated dimensions

queries.query: What are the scaling strategies and performance tuning considerations for vector search using pgvector at production scale?
expected_snippets: reduce the number of rows in index and reduce the concurrent queries
Are you setting your `list` size appropriately for your data set?
pgvector 0.8.0 and iterative scans work as expected

queries.query: What techniques exist for enriching document chunks with surrounding context before indexing to improve RAG retrieval accuracy?
expected_snippets: prepending chunk-specific explanatory context to each chunk before embedding
only afterwards dividing these contextually-rich embeddings into chunks
encodes each hypothetical document into an embedding vector and averages them
